summary: Cut production Workbench over to React/TypeScript/pnpm.
rationale: The approved rebuild is a breaking-change replacement of the retired legacy UI. This slice intentionally deletes the retired frontend app, adds the React Agent Workbench, packages the React build for FastAPI static serving, and wires Storybook, visual regression, cutover gates, and CI checks around the new frontend contract.
dependency_rationale: The production frontend toolchain changes to React/TypeScript/pnpm. The new React dependency files must be reviewed with the retired frontend deletion because this PR intentionally removes the old runtime path.
verification: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 uv run --group dev python -m pytest tests/test_workbench_static_frontend.py tests/test_react_workbench_cutover_gate.py -q -p no:cacheprovider
PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 uv run --group dev python -m pytest tests/test_agent_workbench_contract.py -q -p no:cacheprovider
CI=1 pnpm --dir apps/web-react exec vitest run src/lib/stream/agentStream.test.ts src/lib/stream/agentStreamReducer.test.ts src/lib/stream/agentStreamView.test.ts --run
deletion_targets: retired production UI and old frontend toolchain
docs/ui.md and stale active UI references
old node detail panel UI contract
risks: React production code must not import Storybook/test fixtures.
The packaged static app must come from the React build and must not retain retired runtime assets.
Storybook visual baselines are generated review inputs and must not be treated as handwritten source.
CI cutover gates must reject stale active legacy UI references, including archive material.
